title: Xtra Crumbly Italian Pasta Carbonara
description: A fermented, crumbly, one-pot Italian dish featuring strong white flour, agemono-deep-fry, and raw-friendly ingredients in a carbonara form, this recipe is a unique twist on the classic pasta dish. It combines the richness of eggs and parmesan with the crunch of fried elements.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti according to the package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta is cooking, whisk together the eggs, parmesan cheese, and a pinch of black pepper in a medium bowl.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and fry the guanciale slices until crispy.
Remove the guanciale from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Dredge the fried guanciale in the all-purpose flour, shaking off excess.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the bowl with the egg mixture and toss to combine.
Add the flour-dredged guanciale to the spaghetti and egg mixture, tossing to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional parmesan cheese if desired.
